title: papaver pulvinatum
intro: Papaver pulvinatum, also known as the Armenian poppy, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Papaveraceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant contains alkaloids like thebaine and codeine, which have been used for their medicinal properties, including pain relief and cough suppression. The health benefits of Papaver pulvinatum are attributed to its analgesic, antitussive, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as respiratory issues and muscle spasms. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, or dried extracts, although its cultivation and use are heavily regulated due to its potential for abuse.
